preamble.summary: Joseph grows old and charges his brothers to carry his bones to Canaan. Joseph dies at one hundred and ten. A new king arises who does not know Joseph.
preamble.remarkable: The transition from patriarchal era to oppression. Joseph's bone-request creates a thread resolved at the Exodus (Exodus 13:19).
preamble.friction: Jubilees compresses the timeline between Joseph's death and the oppression.

verses.text_reference: And Joseph dwelt in Egypt after his father died.
verses.rendering: After his father's death, Joseph continued to live in Egypt. He remained ruler over the land.

verses.text_reference: And Joseph died at one hundred and ten years old.
verses.rendering: Joseph died at one hundred and ten. He had lived seventeen years in Canaan, ten years in bondage, three in prison, and eighty ruling Egypt.
translator_notes: The breakdown totals 110 years, matching Genesis 50:26.

verses.text_reference: And he died, and all his brethren, and all that generation.
verses.rendering: He died, as did all his brothers and that entire generation.

verses.text_reference: And he commanded them to carry his bones when they went forth from Egypt.
verses.rendering: Before he died, he commanded the children of Israel to carry his bones with them when they departed from Egypt.

verses.text_reference: And he made them swear, for he knew the Egyptians would try to keep his bones.
verses.rendering: He made them take an oath, for he knew the Egyptians would try to prevent it. Makamaron, king of Egypt, tried to mix Joseph's bones with Egyptian bones.
translator_notes: Makamaron is unique to Jubilees — not found in the Bible or Egyptian records.

verses.text_reference: But they did not find the bones; Joseph's servants had buried them secretly.
verses.rendering: But they could not find Joseph's bones, for his servants had buried them in a secret location.

verses.text_reference: And the bones remained in Egypt for many years.
verses.rendering: Joseph's bones remained in Egypt until the children of Israel departed.

verses.text_reference: And a new king arose who knew not Joseph.
verses.rendering: A new king arose over Egypt who had not known Joseph. He devised a cruel plan against the Israelites and subjected them to harsh forced labor.
translator_notes: This verse marks the transition from the patriarchal era to the Egyptian bondage.

verses.text_reference: And the Lord delivered Israel, as it is written in the first Torah.
verses.rendering: The Lord delivered the children of Israel and brought them out of Egypt, as it is written in the book of the first Torah that Moses wrote.
translator_notes: 'The book of the first Torah' — Jubilees positions itself as supplementary to the Pentateuch.
